meaning: adj.温和的,柔和的,乏味的,冷漠的,刺激性少的
examples: It sounds like a commercial: easy on the ear but bland and forgettable.
To make the bland taste more interesting, the locals began perking it up with local produce.
This is not to say that he is bland.
When Vice was filming, producers noticed the decaying Art Deco buildings, and also their bland, beige colours.
Mr Hollande's performance was "often bland, almost banal", the editorial by Eric Decouty said.
